title: T-002 — Extend settings-owned flat permission enum (Settings + NPD/Technical workflow gates)
prompt:
# T-002 — Extend settings-owned flat permission enum

## Goal
Append the second SETTINGS_EXT permission group covering schema admin, rule registry, reference CRUD, infrastructure, D365, email, onboarding, security policy, SSO, SCIM, IP allowlist, feature flags, and Settings-owned cross-module workflow permissions.

Settings/Auth owns the permission enum, but not every permission string must start with `settings.`. Cross-module workflow permissions are intentionally namespaced to their consuming domain while remaining Settings/Auth-owned: `npd.released_product_edit.{request,authorize}` and `technical.product_spec.approve`. Export `ALL_SETTINGS_EXT_PERMISSIONS`.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/lib/rbac/permissions.enum.ts [modify]
- apps/web/lib/rbac/permissions.test.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given the enum file is read, when imported, then it exposes settings.schema.{view,edit,promote_l1}; settings.rules.view; settings.reference.{view,edit,import}; settings.infra.{view,edit}; settings.d365.{view,edit,toggle}; settings.email.{view,edit}; settings.onboarding.complete; settings.security.edit; settings.sso.{view,edit}; settings.scim.{view,edit}; settings.ip_allowlist.{view,edit}; settings.flags.{view,edit}; settings.authorization.{view,edit}; npd.released_product_edit.{request,authorize}; technical.product_spec.approve.
- Given the file is parsed, when checked, then all new permission strings match the allowed flat namespace regex `^(settings\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_0-9]+|npd\.released_product_edit\.(request|authorize)|technical\.product_spec\.approve)$` and zero duplicates exist.
- Given vitest runs `apps/web/lib/rbac/permissions.test.ts`, when executed, then ALL_SETTINGS_EXT_PERMISSIONS array length equals exactly the count of new strings declared.
- Cross-module workflow permission strings are present and grouped in a Settings/Auth Workflow Authorization section; no rejected 4-level matrix or derived `module_permission_levels` view is introduced.

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Add a failing test that exercises the acceptance criteria above, including the mixed namespace regex and duplicate detection. The RED command is:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/lib/rbac/permissions.test.ts`
2. Run RED — it must fail before any production code is written.
3. Implement the smallest change needed for GREEN.
4. Re-run RED command — must pass.
5. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not require every permission to start with `settings.`; the explicit NPD/Technical workflow permission namespaces are valid and Settings/Auth-owned.
- Do not introduce permissions outside the listed Settings-owned namespaces without a PRD amendment.
- Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components.
- Do not grant `npd.released_product_edit.authorize` to `npd_manager` by default; authorization is org-policy controlled.
- Do not weaken existing acceptance criteria from prior tasks.
- Do not introduce REST endpoints; use Server Actions per stack contract.

pipeline_inputs.description: Extend settings-owned flat permission enum with Settings submodule strings plus explicit NPD/Technical workflow authorization permissions.
pipeline_inputs.details: Same conventions as T-001. Group with delimiter comments (Schema, Rules, Reference, Infra, D365, Email, Onboarding, Security, SSO, SCIM, IP Allowlist, Flags, Workflow Authorization). Include JSDoc per group with PRD section refs. Workflow Authorization contains settings.authorization.view/edit, npd.released_product_edit.request, npd.released_product_edit.authorize, and technical.product_spec.approve. These are Settings/Auth-owned permission strings even when consumed by 01-NPD and 03-TECHNICAL. Valid regex is mixed-namespace and must allow these explicit non-settings prefixes; keep the flat dot-namespaced model.
acceptance_criteria: All listed strings present; mixed-namespace regex passes; uniqueness verified.

Cross-module workflow permission strings are present and grouped: settings.authorization.view/edit, npd.released_product_edit.request/authorize, technical.product_spec.approve; no 4-level matrix introduced.
test_strategy: RED: extend permissions.test.ts for presence, duplicate detection, and mixed-namespace regex.
risk_red_lines: Do not require every permission to start with settings.; explicit npd.released_product_edit.* and technical.product_spec.approve strings are valid Settings/Auth-owned workflow permissions.
Do not introduce permissions outside the listed Settings-owned namespaces without a PRD amendment.
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components.
Do not grant npd.released_product_edit.authorize to npd_manager by default; authorization is org-policy controlled.
